issues.title: React imports appear after third-party imports
issues.description: The import order rule requires React → Third-party → Shared → Feature → Types. In SellerReportsPage.tsx, React (`useCallback, useMemo, useState`) is imported on line 5, after `lucide-react` (line 3) and `next-intl` (line 4). React imports must come first.
issues.fix: Move `import { useCallback, useMemo, useState } from 'react'` to the top before third-party imports.

issues.title: Non-exported module-level constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE instead of camelCase
issues.description: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE is reserved for exported global constants. `MUTED_STYLE` (line 8) and `WARNING_STYLE` (line 9) are non-exported module-level constants. Per naming conventions, non-exported constants should use camelCase: `mutedStyle` and `warningStyle`.
issues.fix: Rename `MUTED_STYLE` to `mutedStyle` and `WARNING_STYLE` to `warningStyle` throughout the file.

issues.title: Non-exported module-level constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE instead of camelCase
issues.description: `ORDERS_HEADERS` (line 7), `ISO_DATE_LENGTH` (line 23), and `ISO_DATETIME_LENGTH` (line 24) are non-exported module-level constants. Per naming conventions, only exported globals should use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE; non-exported constants should use camelCase: `ordersHeaders`, `isoDateLength`, `isoDatetimeLength`.
issues.fix: Rename to `ordersHeaders`, `isoDateLength`, and `isoDatetimeLength` throughout the file.

issues.title: Module-level local variable `KEY` uses SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE inconsistently
issues.description: `const KEY = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` on line 13 is a non-exported module-level constant. While the other constants in the file (`SUPABASE_URL`, `SELLER_EMAIL`, `IMG_BASE`) consistently use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (which is acceptable for module-level config constants in scripts), `KEY` is an overly terse and ambiguous name. It should either follow the same pattern as the others (`SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` or `SUPABASE_KEY`) or be renamed to be more descriptive (`supabaseKey` if camelCase is preferred).
issues.fix: Rename `KEY` to `SUPABASE_KEY` or `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` to match the descriptive pattern used by the other module-level constants in the file.

issues.title: Variable `qs` is an unexpressive abbreviation
issues.description: On line 23, `const qs = toQueryString(filters)` uses a two-letter abbreviation `qs` for `queryString`. While common, abbreviated names reduce readability. A more descriptive name like `queryString` or `queryParams` follows the camelCase convention while being self-documenting.
issues.fix: Rename `qs` to `queryString` or `queryParams` throughout the function.

issues.title: Non-exported module-level constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
issues.description: `APP_ORDER` (line 31), `ADMIN_PERMISSIONS` (line 41), and `APP_ACCESS_RULES` (line 48) are not exported from the file. Per naming conventions, non-exported constants should use camelCase. However, these are effectively configuration constants at module scope and SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE is widely accepted for module-level config even when not exported. This is a low-priority observation.
issues.fix: Consider renaming to `appOrder`, `adminPermissions`, `appAccessRules` for strict compliance, or export them to justify the SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention.
